description: Create one friendly, lightweight Planning Your Event route that centralizes confirmed venue and operational information using progressive disclosure. Link to it sparingly from relevant pages so Home and show pages stay emotional and uncluttered. Do not publish unresolved cancellation, safety, supervision, vendor, insurance, or media-permission claims.
non_goals: Do not create a technical rider, legal contract, quote calculator, or downloadable school document
Do not repeat the complete FAQ on Home, show pages, School Shows, Birthdays, or Booking
Do not publish audience-size price tiers, deposit-refund rules, penalties, fixed travel fees, or guaranteed discounts
Do not claim that adult supervision is unnecessary or that unrestricted physical contact is allowed
Do not publish the owner's private address or unverified school/vendor documentation
Do not redesign the global shell or alter the core site architecture beyond adding this route and minimal links

decision_basis.origin: User-approved seven-task implementation plan after live-site review and owner questionnaire review on 2026-06-27.
investigation_findings: User requested a separate home for technical information so marketing pages remain light
Confirmed operational facts include 15–20 minute setup and pack-down, about 215 sq ft recommended clear space, own sound, usually no power requirement, and indoor/outdoor venue flexibility
Outdoor performances require a level surface, cover/shade as appropriate, and no rain or strong wind
Parking is normally self-managed, but paid parking may be charged to the client
Parents, teachers, and organizers may take their own photos; performers generally do not photograph children
Cancellation and rescheduling answers remain contradictory and must not be converted into a hard public policy

must_not_violate: Page title and tone must be friendly: Planning Your Event, not Technical Requirements
Only confirmed facts may be presented as current requirements
Recommendations must be distinguishable from hard requirements
No public price details beyond From $350
No unsafe child-supervision or physical-contact statements
Use existing design-system primitives and accordion behavior

forbidden_execution_patterns: No dense all-open wall of text on initial render
No duplicate FAQ schema on pages that do not render the same visible questions
No invented exact heat threshold, ceiling height, floor-plan rule, chair requirement, or arrival guarantee
No legal-policy drafting beyond soft booking-change language approved by canon
No footer/nav expansion that makes the route look like a primary service line
execution_guardrails: Use accordion-based progressive disclosure and concise answers
Place one short intro above the accordion and one booking CTA below it
Add only contextual text links from relevant routes and a low-priority footer link
Use FAQPage schema only for questions visibly rendered on this route
If a policy fact is unresolved, use 'confirmed during booking' or omit it

readiness_notes: Confirmed operational facts and page-content boundaries must be canonical

in_scope: Create metadata, breadcrumb, visible FAQ accordions, FAQ schema, and final booking CTA
Sections: space/setup, indoor/outdoor/weather, sound/power, parking/access, photos, multiple performances, and soft booking-change guidance
Add minimal contextual links from School Shows, Birthdays, Booking, show-detail template, and footer
Add route-level e2e coverage and accessibility checks
out_of_scope: School technical rider or teacher guide

steps.title: Define the public question set
actions: Write concise answers with required/recommended language separated
Exclude or soften unresolved policy topics
steps.expectation: The content set is useful but not legally or operationally overcommitted

steps.title: Build the Planning Your Event route
actions: Create the route using existing Container, Section, SectionHeader, Breadcrumb, Accordion, Button, and JsonLd primitives
Use friendly introductory copy and accordion groups
Add a final CTA to booking
steps.expectation: The route matches Lantern Light and is easy to scan on mobile

steps.title: Add structured data and metadata
actions: Add unique metadata and canonical URL
Generate BreadcrumbList and FAQPage only from the visible question data
Keep the route subject to the current pre-launch noindex state
steps.expectation: Metadata and schema reflect visible content exactly

steps.title: Add sparse contextual links
actions: Add one short relevant link from School Shows, Birthdays, Booking, and the show-detail template
Do not add the route as a top-level primary service navigation item
steps.expectation: Detailed information is discoverable without cluttering primary journeys

steps.title: Test progressive disclosure and accessibility
actions: Add/extend e2e tests for route access, accordion state, keyboard use, CTA, and contextual links
Check heading order, focus, labels, schema, and mobile layout
steps.expectation: The route is accessible, lightweight, and regression-safe

success_criteria: /planning-your-event exists and uses friendly progressive disclosure
The route contains only confirmed venue and operational information
Home and show pages do not duplicate the detailed FAQ
Contextual links are present but visually secondary
No audience-size prices, fixed travel fees, deposit rules, penalties, or unsafe supervision statements are published
Visible FAQ content and FAQPage schema match
All required commands and route tests pass

manual_checks: Inspect the route on mobile and desktop with all accordions closed and open
Confirm the page feels like event guidance, not an engineering specification
Confirm contextual links do not dominate Home, show, School, Birthday, or Booking pages
Confirm no unresolved cancellation/safety/vendor/media claim leaked into the page
Validate rendered JSON-LD against the visible questions

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, and deliverables are complete; confirm execution-safety compliance (no non_goals fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns violated, execution_guardrails respected)
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands explicitly listed for the scoped task, confirm all pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure code review — analyze all changes against the review dimensions, record explicit findings with BLOCKER / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NOTE severity
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or explicitly defer findings with severity, write the final verification summary, and update the task report
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — append the substantive change entry only after all previous closure steps pass

post_actions: Do not add detailed policy sections until owner and legal decisions are separately locked
Use this route as the single public source for shared venue requirements
